summary_one_line: Buffer must hold the 3-glyph Devanagari conjunct RA(0x0930)+VIRAMA(0x094D)+I(0x0907) as consecutive codepoints to take case 0x0930's true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Three consecutive buffer codepoints encoding 0x0930 (RA) at the current glyph, then 0x094D (virama) at cur(1), then 0x0907 at cur(2), with idx+2 < count so the third glyph exists. In the harness input these appear as little-endian 32-bit glyph words 30 09 00 00 / 4d 09 00 00 followed by the third codepoint slot.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins 30 09 00 00 4d 09 00 00 06 00 00 00 — the first glyph word is 0x0930 (entering case 0x0930) and the second word is 0x094D (the virama required by cur(1)), supplying the leading two operands of the three-way conjunction that the loser never satisfies; the winner takes the true side (L94 W T=1 F=14 vs L T=0 F=149).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry unrelated bytes at those slots (e.g. 19 09 00 00, 00 00 00 00, sn-hant/zh- ASCII tag strings) so they never co-locate 0x0930, 0x094D and 0x0907 at adjacent glyph positions; across 10 loser trials case 0x0930 stays false (L94 L T=0 F=149) and the branch is blocked for 18.80h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog's I2S. The blocker is a conjunction of three exact 32-bit codepoint equalities; CMP_MAP's Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards inputs that match MORE of the three operands partially (e.g. getting cur()==0x0930 and cur(1)==0x094D before cur(2)==0x0907), giving an intermediate-credit gradient that cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot see, so vpc climbs the multi-equality gate that cmplog stalls on.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker true side requires a three-codepoint conjunction: cur(1)==0x094D, idx+2<count, and cur(2)==0x0907.
The blocker line is case 0x0930u, so the current glyph codepoint must equal 0x0930 to enter this arm.
The winner-resolving seed encodes 0x0930 then 0x094D as the first two little-endian glyph words.
Loser-blocking seeds carry unrelated bytes / ASCII tag strings at the codepoint slots instead of the conjunct.
Winner takes the true side while all loser trials keep case 0x0930 false.
The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over cmplog with the value_profile technique delta.
value_profile_cmplog feedback adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of the edge-bucket and CMP buffer signals.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog feedback is the same edge-bucket signal as naive, with no value-profile gradient.
The loser stays blocked far longer than the winner resolves.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Decisive delta is value_profile (value_profile_cmplog > cmplog), so the winning technique is CMP_MAP gradient, not I2SRandReplace; lineage dash-row verification is mandatory only for I2SRandReplace and is therefore not required here. Both involved fuzzers already carry I2S, so I2S cannot be the differentiator; the only added capability in the winner is the CMP_MAP value-profile gradient feedback.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose blocker is a single exact 32-bit equality (one operand, not a conjunction) shows no value_profile_cmplog advantage over cmplog at the gate, indicating the divergence is not driven by partial-match CMP_MAP gradient over a multi-operand conjunction.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is shown, so the claimed 0x0907 third-operand placement at the cur(2) slot is inferred from the conjunction source rather than directly read off a distinct byte offset in that seed.
